meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve no understanding of APIs (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Client Workflow to Solve No API Knowledge
content.seo_description: Master complex API integrations without deep technical debt. A professional workflow for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: Bridge the API knowledge gap by abstracting backend logic into modular, reusable service layers. Use Antigravity to scaffold robust API clients and Claude Code to automate implementation. This workflow transforms complex endpoint documentation into clean, client-ready production code, ensuring you deliver high-performance integrations without needing to be an infrastructure expert.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a 50-page API documentation file, the client is asking for a 'simple' integration by Friday, and your codebase feels like a fragile house of cards. The gap between a creative vision and a functional, authenticated API request is where most freelance projects go to die. You don't need a CS degree to ship; you need a system that abstracts the complexity.

This workflow is your tactical advantage. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative execution, you stop fighting with JSON payloads and start building high-value features. We aren't just writing code; we are engineering a production-grade interface that treats external APIs as reliable, predictable services.

steps.title: Define the Service Interface
steps.description: Before writing a single line of implementation, define the data contract required by your UI. This prevents leaking API-specific logic into your frontend components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an interface definition for a REST API client that handles authentication headers and standardizes error responses for a SaaS integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate typescript interfaces for the API schema provided in ./docs/api.json'

steps.title: Abstract Authentication Layers
steps.description: Auth is the most common point of failure. Create a centralized provider that handles token rotation and header injection automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a secure middleware pattern for API requests that handles Bearer token injection and automatic 401 retry logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement an axios interceptor in ./src/lib/api-client.ts to handle token injection and 401 refreshes'

steps.title: Automate Endpoint Mapping
steps.description: Instead of manually creating every fetch call, use Claude Code to map your documentation schema to your service layer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a CRUD service module that maps the documentation endpoints to the previously defined interfaces.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'map all endpoints from ./docs/api.json to service methods in ./src/services/api-service.ts'

steps.title: Implement Resilient Error Handling
steps.description: Production code must fail gracefully. Implement a standardized error wrapper that translates API errors into human-readable UI feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a standardized error handling function that parses API error codes into custom application exceptions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'refactor all service methods to use the centralized error handler in ./src/utils/errors.ts'

steps.title: Validation and Integration Testing
steps.description: Verify your integration with mock responses to ensure your UI handles loading states and data transformations correctly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of mock integration tests that validate the API service layer against the defined interface contracts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate vitest unit tests for the api-service.ts module using mock data'

pro_tips: Always wrap external API calls in a Circuit Breaker pattern to prevent cascading failures in your client's application.
Use Zod for runtime schema validation on all incoming API responses to catch breaking changes before they hit your UI.
Cache your API definitions locally; never hit the live documentation site during development to maintain high speed.
Implement request logging that strips out PII or sensitive tokens before printing to your development console.
Use a 'Feature Flag' approach for new API integrations so you can toggle them off without deploying new code.
content.next_steps: Once your API layer is stable, focus on implementing a caching layer (like TanStack Query) to optimize performance and reduce unnecessary API calls.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to handle any integration, regardless of complexity. Stop fearing the documentation and start shipping high-value features that keep your clients coming back for more. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
